exemplar.title: How does temperature (30Â°C to 70Â°C) affect the rate of decomposition of 50mL  0.22 M  nitric acid (HNOâ) in the presence of 0.5 g manganese(IV) oxide (MnOâ) catalyst, measured by the volume of nitrogen dioxide (NOâ) produced over 5 minutes Using a 50 cm3 gas syringe?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does temperature (30Â°C to 70Â°C) affect the rate of decomposition of 50mL  0.22 M  nitric acid (HNOâ) in the presence of 0.5 g manganese(IV) oxide (MnOâ) catalyst, measured by the volume of nitrogen dioxide (NOâ) produced over 5 minutes Using a 50 cm3 gas syringe?

strengths: Raw-data and processed-data tables are clearly laid out with headings and units
Inclusion of averages, standard deviations and an ANOVA demonstrates higher-level processing
Graphical presentation of data enhances clarity
weaknesses: Typographical errors and inconsistent significant figures reduce precision
Uncertainties are calculated for apparatus but not propagated into rates or final analysis
No error bars on graphs; outlier treatment is not justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is generally clear, and the student applies statistical tools. However, major inconsistencies, limited uncertainty treatment and lack of error bars mean the work straddles bands 3-4; a score of 4 reflects solid processing with room to improve precision and uncertainty integration.

strengths: Focused research question embedded in a clear, specific context of nitric-acid decomposition and catalytic action
Step-wise methodology with apparatus table and quantities that largely support reproducibility
Thoughtful identification and control of key variables and repeat measurements
weaknesses: Ambiguity around use of a vacuum pump and pressure control during gas collection
Inconsistent reporting of reaction time (5 min vs 10 min) and erroneous time-unit notation
Incorrect unit for acid concentration (mol dmâ»Â³ miswritten as mol dmâ»Â²)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the context and research question are well framed (band 5-6) and apparatus/variables are described, several ambiguities in procedure and time/pressure control prevent full reproducibility. This places the work at the upper end of band 3-4 moving into band 5, meriting 5 marks.

strengths: Conclusion quantitatively references observed trends and data points
Relevant connection to collision theory and catalytic lowering of activation energy
Logical consistency between data and proposed mechanism
weaknesses: No comparison to literature values or industrial benchmarks
Uncertainty and error discussions are omitted in the conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is supported by the analysis and offers scientific context, placing it toward the top of band 3-4. To reach higher bands the student would need deeper contextual comparison and integration of uncertainties, hence 4 marks.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (e.g., gas-volume measurement, catalyst degradation) are identified
Realistic improvements (digital syringe, magnetic stirring, expanded temperature range) are proposed
Each improvement is linked to how it would enhance reliability
weaknesses: No analysis of the relative impact of each limitation on overall validity
Improvement explanations, while relevant, remain brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific limitations and realistic improvements, justifying a strong performance in band 3-4. A deeper exploration of relative impacts would be required for the top band, so 4 marks are awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a focused RQ and embeds it in a clear background discussion of nitric-acid decomposition, catalytic action of MnOâ and industrial relevance. Sources are cited and the specific concentration, temperature range and measurement technique are all linked to the chemistry involved.  The context is therefore both specific and appropriate, satisfying the descriptors for the top band, though occasional phrasing errors prevent full sophistication; a score of 5 within the 5-6 band is most fitting.

Averages, standard deviations, rates and an ANOVA are provided, but the underlying raw data contain an obvious outlier, the average for 30 Â°C is mis-calculated and the ANOVA p-value is mis-typed.  These inaccuracies constitute âmajor omissions or inconsistencies,â aligning with the 1-2 band; a score of 2 recognises that some correct processing is still present.

Raw-data and processed-data tables are present with headings, units and a graphical display, so the communication is generally clear.  Precision suffers from typographical errors (e.g., 33.5 cmÂ³ mis-entered for 30 Â°C, inconsistent significant figures), reducing precision but not clarity; hence the strand meets âclear OR preciseâ rather than âboth,â fitting the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

For each limitation the student proposes realistic improvements (digital syringe, expanded temperature range, magnetic stirring).  Explanations focus on how the change would help but remain brief, matching the descriptor for âdescribes relevant realistic improvements.â  This warrants 3 marks within the 3-4 band.

Uncertainties of major apparatus are listed and percentage uncertainty for volume is calculated, but propagation to rates or final conclusions is absent.  No uncertainty bars are shown on the graph and random-error discussion is minimal.  This limited treatment corresponds to the 1-2 band; the presence of some correct calculations justifies the upper mark of 2.

The student links findings to collision theory and catalytic lowering of activation energy, giving some scientific context.  No comparison to literature values or deeper mechanistic discussion is offered.  This limited yet relevant comparison fulfils the 3-4 descriptor; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

Specific weaknessesâgas-volume measurement, catalyst degradation, limited temperature rangeâare identified and linked to potential effects, but the relative impact on validity or reliability is not analysed in depth.  This satisfies âdescribes specific weaknesses,â positioning the work in the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

The plan recognises the need for five temperature levels and five repeats and lists several control variables with justification.  However, the rationale for choosing the 5-minute reaction window, the syringe volume limit and the suitability of a water bath in the presence of toxic NOâ are not discussed.  Relevant considerations are present but not fully explained, so the work aligns with âdescribedâ rather than âfully explained,â meriting the lower end (3) of the 3-4 band.

Step-wise instructions, apparatus tables and quantities are provided and would allow another student to repeat the investigation, but with several ambiguities: the use of a âvacuum pumpâ on a gas syringe is unclear, the gas-collection time is inconsistently reported (5 vs 10 min), and pressure control is not specified.  These shortcomings move the description away from complete reproducibility, placing it in the middle of the 3-4 band (score 3).

The conclusion restates the observed positive correlation and references quantitative values from the data table and graph, showing a reasonable match to the analysis.  Because the underlying data include errors and uncertainty has not been fully integrated, the support is only partially secure, meeting the descriptor âdescribed but not fully consistent,â earning 3 within the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The background section concisely explains the role of MnOâ as a catalyst, demonstrating clear linkages to activation energy and surface collisions.
Citation format is inconsistent (e.g., âLibre texts, 2021â). Use a consistent style and include author names and publication info to strengthen the literature context.
The research question statement is incomplete, ending abruptly at âUsing aâ. Clarify the measurement method and ensure the RQ fully communicates the experimental approach.
The title clearly specifies the chemical system, catalyst and variable, showing strong contextualisation of the study within nitric-acid decomposition.
Unit for acid concentration is incorrectly given as mol/dmÂ². Correct to mol dmâ»Â³ to avoid confusion in reagent preparation.
The use of a vacuum pump on a gas syringe is not standard. Clarify why this is necessary and describe how pressure is controlled during gas collection.
Mentioning enzyme denaturation when discussing water-bath temperatures is not relevant to this inorganic reaction. Replace with discussion of catalyst stability.
The dependent-variable description states measurements over ten minutes but elsewhere uses five minutes. Resolve this inconsistency to ensure reproducibility.
The time interval notation âT=â5 minutes (300 minutes)â is erroneous. It should read 300 seconds or 5 minutesâclarify the units used in rate calculations.
Control of stirring is described only as manual rod mixing. Consider using a magnetic stirrer for consistency across trials.
The apparatus table lists âmercury Thermometerâ with inconsistent capitalization and missing precision context. Standardize terminology and detail uncertainty.
Typographical error: âNâOââ instead of noâ in step 5 of the procedure. Correct chemical notation to maintain clarity.
The risk description for NOâ refers to eye exposure forming nitric acid. Highlight inhalation toxicity and respiratory controls instead.
An outlier of 33.50 cmÂ³ at 30 Â°C skews the mean. Identify and justify any data points excluded to improve transparency.
Inconsistent significant figures in averages (e.g., 19.60 vs 19.6). Apply a uniform number of decimal places to all processed data.
PV = nRT calculation mixes units (cmÂ³, kPa, dmÂ³). Convert volume consistently and specify unit conversions to avoid calculation errors.
The standard deviation reported (0.79) contradicts the sample dispersion. Recalculate using raw data and document the formula for clarity.
ANOVA table P-value notation â6.21 x 10-9â lacks superscript formatting. Use correct scientific notation (6.21Ã10â»â¹) to maintain precision.
Explanation of ANOVA results conflates p-value and F-value significance. Separate discussion of statistical thresholds and effect sizes for clarity.
The rate table should label temperature column as Â°C and include units for average volume. Clear headings improve data interpretation.
The graph lacks error bars to represent experimental uncertainty. Adding these will visually communicate data variability.
Only one percentage uncertainty is calculated (%=0.5â4). Propagate uncertainties into rates and include random error discussion.
The uncertainty table lists 23.62 cmÂ³ for 60 Â°C, conflicting with earlier average of 23.30 cmÂ³. Ensure consistency between data sets.
The qualitative discussion largely repeats kinetic theory. Integrate insight from uncertainty analysis or literature values to deepen interpretation.
Using the molar volume of 24 dmÂ³ molâ»Â¹ assumes standard conditions which differ from experimental temperature and pressure. Justify or adjust this approximation.
Limitation to 20 Â°C increments up to 100 Â°C is unrealistic for this reaction due to acid volatility. Propose smaller increments or alternative methods.
The concluding summary restates results but omits comparison with industrial or literature benchmarks. Include such comparisons to strengthen context.
The ScienceDirect reference lacks author names, article title and publication details. Complete the citation to meet academic standards.
The raw-data table is well-structured with clear headings, units and trial numbers, facilitating transparent communication of primary observations.
